title: Global Headlines: Conflicts, Politics, and Personal News
summary: Today's podcast covers tragic events in global conflict zones, key political updates from the US, a puzzling murder investigation, and news from the world of golf.
script:
Hello everyone, and welcome to 'Daily Dose of News.' I'm your host, and today we're covering a lot of important stories from around the world. We'll start with tragic news from Lebanon and Iraq, and a very disturbing report from Iran. Then, we'll shift to US news about the White House and some military pilots. We'll also look at a puzzling case in Italy and an update on a famous golfer. So, let's get right into today's headlines and learn what's happening.

First, to Lebanon, with very sad news. Two peacekeepers from Indonesia have died. They were killed by a roadside bomb, meaning a bomb planted on the side of the road exploded. This terrible event happened less than a day after another Indonesian UN peacekeeper was also killed in Lebanon. So, three Indonesian peacekeepers have tragically died very recently. These brave individuals serve with the United Nations mission, working to maintain peace in dangerous areas. Our thoughts are truly with their families, their colleagues, and their country during this incredibly difficult time.

Also concerning Lebanon, Israel has announced it will keep control over a part of southern Lebanon. This decision will stand even after the war with Hezbollah ends. Israel's Defence Minister, Israel Katz, also said something very serious: that houses in Lebanese villages near the Israeli border will be destroyed. This means they will be torn down. This is a very significant decision and will deeply affect many people living in those areas. It certainly highlights the ongoing tensions and serious challenges in the region.

Now, let's move to Iraq, where US journalist Shelly Kittleson has been kidnapped in Baghdad. This is a very serious and worrying situation. The US State Department says a suspect in her abduction has ties to Kataib Hezbollah. This group is known as an Iran-backed militia. A 'militia' is like a military force that is not part of the official army, and 'Iran-backed' means it gets support from Iran. We are all hoping for her safe and swift return home.

From Iran, we have a deeply troubling report. It states that children are being used in security roles during war. Witnesses and various reports confirm this. A specific and tragic detail has emerged: an 11-year-old child was reportedly killed in an air strike. This happened while the child was manning a checkpoint in Tehran. This shocking news truly highlights the very sad and dangerous situation for children caught in conflict zones, who should be safe.

Let's move to the United States. A judge has temporarily stopped the building of a new ballroom at the White House. This ballroom was planned by President Trump. The judge, who was appointed by Republicans, ruled that Trump is the 'steward' of the White House, not its 'owner.' 'Steward' means he is responsible for looking after public property for everyone. This important decision means there are clear limits on what changes can be made to this national treasure, ensuring its preservation.

Still in the US, the suspension of some US Army pilots has been officially ended. They were suspended for flying their helicopters near the home of the famous musician Kid Rock. When President Donald Trump was asked about the incident, he simply said, 'They like Kid Rock. I like Kid Rock.' It appears this presidential comment played a role in the pilots getting their jobs back so quickly. It's an interesting turn of events.

Now, to Italy for a very strange and tragic story. Two women died after a Christmas meal. What first seemed like a tragedy is now a murder investigation. Test results suggest these women may have been killed with ricin, a very dangerous poison. Police are actively investigating, but right now, they do not have any suspects. This is a serious and puzzling case from a time usually filled with joy and family gathering.

Finally, news about the famous golfer, Tiger Woods. He has announced he is 'stepping away for a period of time' to 'seek treatment and focus on my health.' This important personal decision comes after he was arrested following a car crash. It's good to see him prioritizing his well-being and seeking professional help. We wish him all the best on his recovery journey.

And that's all for today's 'Daily Dose of News.' We've covered international conflicts, US political updates, and personal stories. Thank you for tuning in and staying informed with us. It's always crucial to know what's happening both near and far. Remember to stay safe, and we'll be back next time with more important news from around the globe. Goodbye for now.
